# Spec — v1 create + single append + full scan. The absolute baseline.
# Authored-golden. The corpus had no clean v1 fixture; this is it. Uses only
# create + append + scan — the narrowest possible write path, green on every
# implementation. A divergence here is a bug in the most basic thing there is.
#
# Pins: format-version 1, the single append snapshot (ordinal 0, no parent),
# its append operation + aggregate summary counts, and the live row multiset.

entries:

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — the int-vs-long distinction (apache/iceberg-go#880), minimal form.
# c_int is int32; c_long is int64 with a value > int32 max, so an implementation
# that conflates the two cannot pass. Authored-golden. Green on the current
# runners (int32/int64 both decode).
#
# The machine-checkable form of #880 lives in the golden: c_int decodes to a
# JSON number, c_long to a JSON string (the 64-bit rule) — and the iceberg-schema
# types "int" vs "long" are themselves an assertion.

header:
  id: v1_int_vs_long
  format-version: 1
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#primitive-types"
  table-uuid: "2c3d4e5f-6071-8293-a4b5-c6d7e8f90112"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: c_int,  type: int,  required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: c_long, type: long, required: true }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, c_int: !int 1,  c_long: !long 1 }             # same numeral, different width
      - { __rowkey: r2, c_int: !int 42, c_long: !long 10000000000 }   # > int32 max: only valid as long
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { c_int: 1,  c_long: 1 }
          - { c_int: 42, c_long: 10000000000 }

# Spec — required vs optional fields and per-column null handling. req is
# required and present on every row; opt_* are optional and null on some rows.
# Restricted to scalar types the runners decode today (boolean/long/double/
# string), so it is green now. Authored-golden.
#
# Pins three distinctions no existing fixture covers: (a) null vs present,
# (b) empty-string "" is a present value distinct from null, (c) 0.0 is a
# present value distinct from null, and it exercises boolean decode.

header:
  id: v1_required_optional_nulls
  format-version: 1
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#nested-types"
  table-uuid: "5f607182-93a4-b5c6-d7e8-f90112233445"

    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"

      - { id: 1, name: req,      type: long,    required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: opt_str,  type: string }
      - { id: 3, name: opt_bool, type: boolean }
      - { id: 4, name: opt_dbl,  type: double }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, req: !long 1, opt_str: !string "present", opt_bool: !boolean true,  opt_dbl: !double 1.5 }
      - { __rowkey: r2, req: !long 2, opt_str: !null null,        opt_bool: !null null,     opt_dbl: !null null }
      - { __rowkey: r3, req: !long 3, opt_str: !string "",        opt_bool: !boolean false, opt_dbl: !double 0.0 }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:

          - { req: 2, opt_str: null,    opt_bool: null,  opt_dbl: null }
          - { req: 3, opt_str: "",      opt_bool: false, opt_dbl: 0.0 }

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — v1 time-travel on a pure-append history, by BOTH ordinal and bind.
# Complements v2_append_timetravel (bind only) by pinning ordinal addressing
# (observe at: 0). Authored-golden, green on the current runners.
#
# Pins that ordinal 0 and bind "after-first" resolve to the identical snapshot.

header:
  id: v1_time_travel_ordinal
  format-version: 1
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#point-in-time-reads"
  table-uuid: "3d4e5f60-7182-93a4-b5c6-d7e8f9011223"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,  type: long, required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: val, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, val: !string "x" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-first

  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, val: !string "y" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  # travel by explicit commit ordinal 0
  - op: observe
    at: 0
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, val: x }

  # travel by bind name to the same point
  - op: observe
    at: after-first
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, val: x }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, val: x }
          - { id: 2, val: y }

golden.note: append 3 rows, merge-on-read positional delete of id=2, then time-travel back to the pre-delete snapshot. One observation per 'observe' entry in the op-log, in order. decoded-rows keyed by field-id (0=__rowkey, 1=id, 2=category); positional delete => delete-file content=1. NOTE: on the delete snapshot, total-records stays 3 (the physical data-record count; the delete is a separate file) and deleted-records is not set — this is the merge-on-read summary semantics, confirmed byte-identical across the iceberg-java reference and iceberg-go. An earlier draft of this golden used logical live-row counts (total-records=2, deleted-records=1); the reference settled it.

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec 1 — v2 append + merge-on-read POSITION delete + time-travel.
# Authored-golden mode (expect present). Green on iceberg-go: MoR delete writes
# position deletes today, which is exactly what this pins.
#
# NOTE: this is the position-delete case, NOT equality. Equality delete is a
# declared gap for iceberg-go (see supports.yaml) — a separate spec would exit 4.

header:
  id: v2_append_posdelete_timetravel
  format-version: 2
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#row-level-deletes"
  table-uuid: "9c12d441-03fe-4693-9a96-a0705ddf69c1"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,       type: long,   required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: category, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, category: !string "a" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, category: !string "b" }
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 3, category: !string "a" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-append
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

  - op: delete
    strategy: merge-on-read
    kind: position
    predicate: { type: eq, term: id, value: !long 2 }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: delete }
      delete-file: { content: 1 }          # positional (not a deletion vector, not equality)

  # rule: mutations assert snapshot facts; observes assert row-state.
  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-delete
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

  # time-travel: the pre-delete snapshot still reads all three rows
  - op: observe
    at: after-append
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

ops.summary: observe · read at the latest snapshot
observe · read at the latest snapshot

golden.note: two appends (ordinals 0 and 1), then time-travel to the first snapshot. Universal-write baseline: create + append + scan + time-travel only, so every implementation runs it green (verified byte-identical modulo row order across iceberg-go and iceberg-rust). decoded-rows keyed by field-id (0=__rowkey, 1=id, 2=category); row order is not significant (the orchestrator sorts by __rowkey).

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — v2 two appends + time-travel, NO delete. Authored-golden.
#
# The universal-write fixture: it uses only create + append + scan + time-travel,
# which every implementation supports. This is the cross-implementation baseline
# — iceberg-go AND iceberg-rust both run it fully green, so a divergence here is a
# real interop bug, not a feature gap. (The sibling v2_append_posdelete_timetravel
# adds a merge-on-read delete, which iceberg-rust cannot write yet -> exit 4.)
#
# Pins: snapshot ordinals across two commits (0, 1), the append operation, live
# row multiset after each append, and a time-travel read of the first snapshot.

header:
  id: v2_append_timetravel
  format-version: 2
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#snapshots"
  table-uuid: "7b3e1a02-9c4d-4f18-8a2b-6e5f0d1c2b3a"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,       type: long,   required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: category, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, category: !string "a" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, category: !string "b" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-first
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }

  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 3, category: !string "a" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-second
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

  # time-travel: the first snapshot still reads only the first two rows
  - op: observe
    at: after-first
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }

ops.summary: observe · read at the latest snapshot

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — v2 merge-on-read delete of ALL rows (predicate always-true). The edge
# case: a delete that empties the table. Authored-golden (minted from
# iceberg-java). Pins that the emptied scan is [] (not an error).
#
# SURFACED DIVERGENCE (intentional red cell for iceberg-go): when a MoR delete
# matches EVERY row, the two writers disagree on the snapshot summary —
#   iceberg-java: writes a positional delete file over the retained data;
#                 total-records: 2 (physical data records unchanged),
#                 total-delete-files: 1.
#   iceberg-go:   shortcuts the whole-table delete to a DATA-FILE REMOVAL (no
#                 delete file); total-records: 0, deleted-records: 2.
# Same logical result (empty table); different physical strategy + metadata. A
# reader keying on total-delete-files sees different things. Golden follows the
# Java reference; iceberg-go fails this cell until the behavior is reconciled.

header:
  id: v2_delete_all_rows
  format-version: 2
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#row-level-deletes"
  table-uuid: "0e1f2a3b-4c5d-6e7f-8091-223344556677"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,  type: long,   required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: val, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, val: !string "a" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, val: !string "b" }

  - op: delete
    strategy: merge-on-read
    kind: position
    predicate:
      type: "true"     # quoted: the always-true predicate tag is the string "true", not a YAML boolean
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: delete }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows: []

ops.summary: observe · read at the latest snapshot

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — v2 delete a row, then append a NEW row with the same user id. Classic
# DML lifecycle: delete-is-set-minus, then a re-insert is a distinct live row
# (new __rowkey). Pins that the position delete does NOT suppress the later
# re-added row (deletes are scoped to the data file+position they targeted, not
# to the id value). Authored-golden (minted from iceberg-java).

header:
  id: v2_delete_then_reappend
  format-version: 2
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#row-level-deletes"
  table-uuid: "1f2a3b4c-5d6e-7f80-9122-334455667788"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,  type: long,   required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: val, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, val: !string "first" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, val: !string "keep" }

  - op: delete
    strategy: merge-on-read
    kind: position
    predicate: { type: eq, term: id, value: !long 1 }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: delete }
      delete-file: { content: 1 }

  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 1, val: !string "second" }   # same id 1, new row

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 2, val: keep }
          - { id: 1, val: second }   # the re-added id=1 survives; the deleted one does not

# Read-conformance fixture — load a checked-in v2 table and observe it.

# source: artifact means the runner does NOT build the table from an op-log; it
# materializes the checked-in bytes/ (restoring them to the pinned root named in
# bytes/ROOT) and loads the table read-only, then runs the observe entries. This
# is the read side of the same contract: the observe -> decode -> canonical-emit
# half is identical to the write (synthesized) fixtures.

# The bytes/ tree was minted by running fixtures/v2_append_timetravel through the
# DML runner into the pinned root — so this fixture and its write sibling share a
# golden's row-state, demonstrating that write-conformance mints read-conformance.

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — v3 add-column where initial-default != write-default. The sharp case
# the equal-defaults fixture (v3_schema_evolution_defaults) can't reach: existing
# rows must read the INITIAL default, while a later append that OMITS the column
# must get the WRITE default. An impl that conflates the two reads wrong historic
# data. Authored-golden (minted from iceberg-java, the reference).
#
# score: initial-default -1 (backfills rows written before the column existed),
# write-default 0 (fills the column when a new row omits it). r1/r2 predate the
# column -> -1; r3 is appended after, omitting score -> 0.

header:
  id: v3_add_column_divergent_defaults
  format-version: 3
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#default-values"
  table-uuid: "6a7b8c9d-0e1f-2a3b-4c5d-6e7f80912233"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,   type: int, required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: name, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !int 1, name: !string "ann" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !int 2, name: !string "bob" }

  - op: evolve-schema
    changes:
      - kind: add-column
        field:
          id: 3
          name: score
          type: int
          initial-default: !int -1     # existing rows (r1, r2) read -1
          write-default: !int 0        # a new row omitting score gets 0

  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !int 3, name: !string "cid" }   # score omitted -> write-default 0

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, name: ann, score: -1 }   # initial-default backfills existing rows
          - { id: 2, name: bob, score: -1 }
          - { id: 3, name: cid, score: 0 }    # write-default fills the omitted column

ops.summary: observe · read at the latest snapshot

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — reads bind by FIELD-ID, never by name. Write rows under {a, b, d}
# (ids 1,2,3); rename field-id 3 (d -> c); then add a NEW column also named "d"
# (fresh id 4). Old rows must surface their id-3 value under the renamed name
# "c", and read NULL for the brand-new "d" (id 4) — they never had id-4 data.
# An impl that resolves columns by name (or reuses physical positions) returns
# the wrong column. Authored-golden (minted from iceberg-java).

header:
  id: v3_rename_then_add_reused_name
  format-version: 3
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#column-projection"
  table-uuid: "7b8c9d0e-1f2a-3b4c-5d6e-7f8091223344"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: a, type: int, required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: b, type: string }
      - { id: 3, name: d, type: int }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, a: !int 1, b: !string "x", d: !int 300 }
      - { __rowkey: r2, a: !int 2, b: !string "y", d: !int 301 }

  # Two SEPARATE commits: a rename and an add-of-the-freed-name cannot share one
  # updateSchema transaction (the conflict guard validates against the original
  # schema, where "d" still exists). Rename first, commit, then add.
  - op: evolve-schema
    changes:
      - kind: rename-column          # field-id 3: d -> c  (id preserved)
        field-id: 3
        to: c

  - op: evolve-schema
    changes:
      - kind: add-column             # a brand-new "d" (fresh id 4), distinct from old id-3
        field:
          id: 4
          name: d
          type: int

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          # old id-3 value now reads under "c"; the new "d" (id 4) is null for old rows
          - { a: 1, b: x, c: 300, d: null }
          - { a: 2, b: y, c: 301, d: null }

ops.summary: observe · read at the latest snapshot
observe · read at the latest snapshot

golden.note: append, then promote id int->long and add column score (long) with initial-default/write-default=0, then append a row omitting score, then time-travel to the pre-evolution snapshot. iceberg-schema differs across observations (evolution). initial-default backfills existing rows; write-default fills the omitted column. Row lineage (_row_id) verified against the current v3 spec before blessing.

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec 3 — v3 schema evolution + default values + time-travel across the change.
# Authored-golden. Green on iceberg-go: UpdateSchema (promote-type, add-column
# with initial-default/write-default).
#
# Row lineage (_row_id / next-row-id) is expressed logically (__rowkey -> row-id)
# rather than by raw reserved field-id; VERIFY the exact reserved ids against the
# current v3 spec before blessing the golden.

header:
  id: v3_schema_evolution_defaults
  format-version: 3
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#schema-evolution"
  table-uuid: "aa11bb22-cc33-dd44-ee55-ff6677889900"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,   type: int, required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: name, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !int 1, name: !string "ann" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !int 2, name: !string "bob" }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: v1

  - op: evolve-schema
    changes:
      - kind: promote-type
        field-id: 1
        to: long
      - kind: add-column
        field:
          id: 3
          name: score
          type: long
          initial-default: !long 0     # existing rows read as 0
          write-default: !long 0       # new rows omitting score get 0

  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 3, name: !string "cid" }   # score omitted -> write-default

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, name: ann, score: 0 }   # initial-default backfills existing rows
          - { id: 2, name: bob, score: 0 }
          - { id: 3, name: cid, score: 0 }   # write-default fills the omitted column
      physical:
        metadata.next-row-id: ">= 3"
        row-lineage: { r1: 0, r2: 1, r3: 2 }

  # pre-evolution snapshot reads the old schema and rows only
  - op: observe
    at: v1
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, name: ann }
          - { id: 2, name: bob }
